dictionary.B36: B36 Tórshavn is a major football club from Tórshavn, founded in 1936 and based at Gundadalur.
dictionary.HB: Havnar Bóltfelag (HB) is a historic Tórshavn football club founded in 1904.
dictionary.KÍ: Klaksvíkar Ítróttarfelag (KÍ) became the first Faroese club to reach a UEFA group stage in 2023.
dictionary.VÍKINGUR: Víkingur is a Faroese football club rooted in Norðragøta and Leirvík.
dictionary.EIVØR: Eivør Pálsdóttir is an internationally known Faroese singer-songwriter blending folk, rock, electronic music, and traditional vocal styles.
dictionary.TEITUR: Teitur Lassen is a Faroese singer-songwriter known internationally for acoustic and indie-pop recordings.
dictionary.TÝR: Týr is a Faroese metal band known for drawing on Norse history, mythology, and traditional ballad melodies.
dictionary.BRIMHEIM: Brimheim is the stage name of Helena Heinesen Rebensdorff, a Faroese-Danish alternative rock performer.
dictionary.MYKINES: Mykines is one of the westernmost Faroese islands and is known for puffins, steep sea cliffs, and its lighthouse area.
dictionary.SUÐUROY: Suðuroy is the southernmost large island in the Faroe Islands and is reached from Tórshavn by ferry.
dictionary.FUGLOY: Fugloy, meaning Bird Island, is a remote northeastern island served by ferry and helicopter.
dictionary.KALSOY: Kalsoy is a long northern island known for narrow road tunnels, the Kallur lighthouse, and the James Bond memorial near Trøllanes.
dictionary.ATLANTIC: Atlantic Airways is the Faroese airline based at Vágar Airport.
dictionary.SMYRIL: Smyril is the domestic ferry serving the Tórshavn-Suðuroy route for Strandfaraskip Landsins.
dictionary.SSL: SSL stands for Strandfaraskip Landsins, the public transport company operating buses and ferries in the Faroe Islands.
dictionary.BUSSLEIÐIN: Bussleiðin is the local bus service in Tórshavn.
dictionary.VÁGATUNNILIN: Vágatunnilin is the subsea tunnel connecting Vágar with Streymoy.
dictionary.NORÐOYATUNNILIN: Norðoyatunnilin connects Borðoy and Eysturoy, linking Klaksvík to the central road network.
dictionary.EYSTUROYARTUNNILIN: Eysturoyartunnilin links Streymoy and Eysturoy and is known for its undersea roundabout.
dictionary.SØRVÁGSVATN: Sørvágsvatn, also called Leitisvatn, is the famous lake on Vágar that appears to sit above the ocean from certain viewpoints.
dictionary.MÚLAFOSSUR: Múlafossur is the well-known waterfall at Gásadalur on Vágar.
dictionary.FOSSÁ: Fossá is the tallest waterfall in the Faroe Islands, located on northern Streymoy.
dictionary.BØSDALAFOSSUR: Bøsdalafossur is the waterfall where Leitisvatn/Sørvágsvatn flows into the Atlantic Ocean.
dictionary.KVÆÐI: Kvæði are traditional Faroese ballads, often sung during the chain dance.
dictionary.SKIPARI: The skipari is the lead singer who starts verses in the Faroese chain dance.
dictionary.STEV: Stev refers to verse, refrain, or rhythm patterns used in Faroese ballad and dance traditions.
dictionary.SKERPIKJØT: Skerpikjøt is wind-dried fermented mutton, traditionally aged in open-air drying sheds called hjallur.
dictionary.TVØST: Tvøst is pilot whale meat, traditionally eaten boiled, salted, or dried.
dictionary.RÆST: Ræst describes a Faroese fermentation stage between fresh and fully dried, used for meat and fish.
dictionary.SPEK: Spek is whale blubber, traditionally preserved and served with whale meat, fish, or potatoes.
dictionary.SLÆTTARATINDUR: Slættaratindur is the highest mountain in the Faroe Islands at 880 metres.
dictionary.GRÁFELLI: Gráfelli is the second-highest mountain in the Faroe Islands, near Slættaratindur on Eysturoy.
dictionary.VILLINGADALSFJALL: Villingadalsfjall rises above Viðareiði on Viðoy and is among the highest Faroese peaks.
dictionary.KÚVINGAFJALL: Kúvingafjall on Kunoy is one of the four highest mountain peaks in the Faroe Islands.
dictionary.ENNIBERG: Cape Enniberg on Viðoy is one of the highest sea cliffs facing the open North Atlantic.
dictionary.TRÆLANÍPAN: Trælanípan, the slave cliff, is a famous viewpoint above Sørvágsvatn on Vágar.
dictionary.FJALL: Fjall is the Faroese word for mountain.
dictionary.DALUR: Dalur means valley and appears in Faroese place names such as Gásadalur.
dictionary.NES: Nes means headland or promontory and appears in several Faroese place names.
dictionary.VÍK: Vík means bay or cove and appears in place names such as Klaksvík and Hvalvík.
dictionary.TINGANES: Tinganes is the historic peninsula in Tórshavn associated with Faroese government and parliament traditions.
dictionary.SKANSIN: Skansin is the historic fort by Tórshavn harbour.
dictionary.SETUR: Setur is short for Fróðskaparsetur Føroya, the University of the Faroe Islands.
dictionary.GLASIR: Glasir is a large educational institution in Tórshavn offering upper secondary, vocational, and higher education.
dictionary.VINNUHÁSKÚLIN: Vinnuháskúlin is the Faroese maritime and engineering school in Tórshavn.
dictionary.FØROYA TELE: Føroya Tele is the public telecommunications company in the Faroe Islands.
dictionary.NEMA: Nema is a Faroese telecom and technology company and one of the two GSM providers listed for visitors.
dictionary.TELEVARPIÐ: Televarpið is a Faroese TV service brand associated with Føroya Tele.
dictionary.FT NET: FT Net is the network infrastructure branch of the Føroya Tele group.
dictionary.TÓRSHAVN: Tórshavn is the capital of the Faroe Islands and the headquarters town of P/F Smyril Line.
dictionary.HIRTSHALS: Hirtshals in Denmark is one of Smyril Line's office and ferry terminal towns.
dictionary.KIEL: Kiel in Germany is one of Smyril Line's office towns.
dictionary.SEYÐISFJØRÐUR: Seyðisfjørður in eastern Iceland is Smyril Line's Icelandic office and Norröna terminal town.
dictionary.MS NORRÖNA: MS Norröna is Smyril Line's passenger and freight ferry sailing between Denmark, the Faroe Islands, and Iceland.
dictionary.MV MYKINES: MV Mykines is one of Smyril Line Cargo's Ro-Ro vessel names.
dictionary.MV GLYVURSNES: MV Glyvursnes is one of Smyril Line Cargo's Ro-Ro vessel names.
dictionary.MV LISTA: MV Lista is listed by Smyril Line Cargo as a Ro-Ro vessel.
dictionary.SKANSAGARÐUR: Skansagarður is one of the dining venues listed onboard MS Norröna.
dictionary.MUNKASTOVA: Munkastova is one of the dining venues listed onboard MS Norröna.
dictionary.NÓATÚN: Nóatún Cafeteria is one of the dining venues onboard MS Norröna.
dictionary.UNDIRHÚSIÐ: Undirhúsið is one of the onboard venues listed by Smyril Line for MS Norröna.
dictionary.HVALVÍK: Hvalvík is a village on Streymoy.
dictionary.OY BREWING: OY Brewing is a Faroese brewery brand listed by Visit Faroe Islands among local beer options.
dictionary.ÓLAVSKIRKJAN: Ólavskirkjan is the medieval St Olav's Church in Kirkjubøur.
dictionary.FÁMJIN: The church in Fámjin is known for preserving the first Faroese flag.
dictionary.PASSING PLACES: Passing places are essential on many narrow Faroese roads and single-lane tunnels.
dictionary.TRØLLANES: Trøllanes is the northern Kalsoy village near the Kallur lighthouse and James Bond memorial area.
dictionary.TVØROYRI: Tvøroyri is a town and main ferry port on Suðuroy.
dictionary.VÁGUR: Vágur is one of the larger villages on Suðuroy.
dictionary.SUMBA: Sumba is the southernmost village in the Faroe Islands.
dictionary.HVALBA: Hvalba is a village on northern Suðuroy known in part for coal-mining history.
dictionary.SANDUR: Sandur is a village on Sandoy.
dictionary.SKOPUN: Skopun is a village on Sandoy.
dictionary.SKÁLAVÍK: Skálavík is a village on Sandoy.
dictionary.HÚSAVÍK: Húsavík is one of the oldest villages on Sandoy.
dictionary.SØRVÁGUR: Sørvágur is a village on Vágar and a gateway to Mykines.
dictionary.MIÐVÁGUR: Miðvágur is a village on Vágar near Sørvágsvatn.
dictionary.SANDAVÁGUR: Sandavágur is a village on Vágar.
dictionary.GÁSADALUR: Gásadalur is the Vágar village beside Múlafossur waterfall.
dictionary.LØGMAÐUR: Løgmaður is the Faroese title for the Prime Minister of the Faroe Islands.
dictionary.LANDSSTÝRI: Føroya Landsstýri is the Government of the Faroe Islands.
dictionary.LØGTINGSFORMAÐUR: Løgtingsformaður is the Speaker of the Løgting.
dictionary.NORÐURLANDAHÚSIÐ: Norðurlandahúsið, the Nordic House, is a cultural centre in Tórshavn.
dictionary.LISTASAVN: Listasavn Føroya is the National Gallery of the Faroe Islands.
dictionary.REINSARÍIÐ: Reinsaríið is a concert venue in central Tórshavn.
dictionary.SALT: SALT is a culture centre in Suðuroy, built from a former salt silo at Drelnes.
dictionary.MERKIÐ: Merkið is the Faroese flag, a white field with a red cross outlined in blue.
dictionary.TJALDUR: Tjaldur, the oystercatcher, is the national bird of the Faroe Islands.
dictionary.ÓLAVSØKA: Ólavsøka is the Faroese national day, held annually on July 29.
dictionary.VEÐRUR: Veðrur, the ram, appears on the Faroese coat of arms.
